ddls:
CREATE TABLE "vehicles" (
  "vehicle_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each vehicle */,
  "vehicle_name" TEXT /* Name of the vehicle */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of vehicle (e.g., car, truck, drone) */,
  "vehicle_ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the vehicle */,
  "vehicle_location" TEXT /* Location of the vehicle (e.g., city, country) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packet_types" (
  "packet_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet type */,
  "packet_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the packet type (e.g., TCP, UDP, ICMP) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the packet type */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used by the packet type (e.g., IPv4, IPv6) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "networks" (
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network */,
  "network_name" TEXT /* Name of the network */,
  "network_type" TEXT /* Type of network (e.g., LAN, WAN, Wi-Fi) */,
  "bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth of the network in Mbps */,
  "latency" REAL /* Latency of the network in ms */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packets" (
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet */,
  "timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of packet transmission */,
  "vehicle_id" TEXT /* ID of the vehicle transmitting the packet */,
  "packet_size" INTEGER /* Size of the packet in bytes */,
  "e2e_delay" REAL /* End-to-end delay of packet transmission */,
  "service_time" REAL /* Service time of packet transmission */,
  "packet_type" TEXT /* Type of packet (e.g., TCP, UDP, ICMP) */,
  "packet_sequence_number" INTEGER /* Sequence number of the packet */,
  "packet_acknowledgment_number" INTEGER /* Acknowledgment number of the packet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_packet_type FOREIGN KEY ("packet_type") REFERENCES packet_types ("packet_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packets_vehicle_id FOREIGN KEY ("vehicle_id") REFERENCES vehicles ("vehicle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_packets" (
  "network_packet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network packet */,
  "network_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network transmitting the packet */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the packet being transmitted */,
  "transmission_time" REAL /* Time of packet transmission */,
  "packet_loss_rate" REAL /* Packet loss rate of the network */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_packet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_packets_network_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_id") REFERENCES networks ("network_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_packets_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES packets ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, engineer, analyst) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the network data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the network data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location of access (e.g., city, country) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packet_analytics" (
  "packet_analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet analytic */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the packet being analyzed */,
  "average_delay" REAL /* Average delay of packet transmission */,
  "max_delay" REAL /* Maximum delay of packet transmission */,
  "min_delay" REAL /* Minimum delay of packet transmission */,
  "packet_loss_rate" REAL /* Packet loss rate of the packet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packet_analytics_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES packets ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_analytics" (
  "network_analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network analytic */,
  "network_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network being analyzed */,
  "average_throughput" REAL /* Average throughput of packet transmission */,
  "max_throughput" REAL /* Maximum throughput of packet transmission */,
  "min_throughput" REAL /* Minimum throughput of packet transmission */,
  "network_latency" REAL /* Latency of the network */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_analytics_network_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_id") REFERENCES networks ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packet_alerts" (
  "packet_alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet alert */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the packet generating the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high delay, packet loss) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packet_alerts_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES packets ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_alerts" (
  "network_alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network alert */,
  "network_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network generating the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high throughput, network congestion) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_alerts_network_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_id") REFERENCES networks ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packet_sequence" (
  "packet_sequence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet sequence */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the packet being sequenced */,
  "sequence_number" INTEGER /* Sequence number of the packet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packet_sequence_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES packets ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packet_acknowledgment" (
  "packet_acknowledgment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet acknowledgment */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the packet being acknowledged */,
  "acknowledgment_number" INTEGER /* Acknowledgment number of the packet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_acknowledgment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packet_acknowledgment_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES packets ("packet_id")
)
